claim: As of May 8, 2026, the three main Algarve public supply dams are near capacity: Odelouca 88.18% full (138.56 hm³), Odeleite 96.43% full (125.36 hm³), Beliche 91.74% full (44.04 hm³) — representing full viable permanent water sources for wildlife and lost dogs.
stance_target: Algarve reservoirs as active permanent water sources in current territorial layer (2026 operational status)
scope_conditions: Three main Algarve dams (Odelouca, Odeleite, Beliche); data from Águas do Algarve weekly monitoring as of 08/05/2026
quoted_support: BARRAGEM DE ODELOUCA: 08/05/26 — 138,56 hm³ (88,18%) total, 109,62 hm³ (85,51%) útil. BARRAGEM DE ODELEITE: 08/05/26 — 125,36 hm³ (96,43%) total, 104,16 hm³ (95,74%) útil. BARRAGEM DE BELICHE: 08/05/26 — 44,04 hm³ (91,74%) total, 38,93 hm³ (90,76%) útil.
numbers: Odelouca total capacity: 157.14 hm³ useful 128.2 hm³

claim: Reservoir levels decreased steadily each week from April to May 2026 (Odelouca: 141.12 hm³ on 10/04/26 → 138.56 hm³ on 08/05/26), confirming summer drawdown trend as evaporation and abstraction exceed rainfall inputs.
stance_target: Algarve reservoir seasonal depletion rate — for temporal modeling of water availability in summer rescue scenarios
scope_conditions: Barragem de Odelouca; April-May 2026 weekly data
quoted_support: 10/04/26: 141,12 hm³ (89,80%) → 08/05/26: 138,56 hm³ (88,18%) — consistent weekly decline across all three reservoirs.
